weather.tip: Mostly clear and mild at 27.9°C this evening with next to no rain risk (2%), so it's a good evening to be outdoors. It'll cool to around 20°C overnight, so keep a light layer handy if you're out late.

around.headline: Bengaluru teacher dies by suicide 4 months after love marriage, alleges dowry harassment
around.summary: A schoolteacher died by suicide months after her love marriage, leaving a note accusing her husband and sister-in-law of harassment, with reports pointing to dowry-related demands. If someone close to you is going through this, gently point them to the Women's Helpline (181) rather than treating it as a private family matter to stay quiet about.
around.source: India Today / The Times of India / Gulf News

around.headline: Flipkart fires delivery agent who barged into Bengaluru woman's flat and exposed himself
around.summary: Flipkart has terminated the delivery agent arrested for pushing his way into a woman's home and exposing himself; he claims he ran off when she started shouting. Verify a delivery person's identity through the app before opening your door fully, and keep it on the chain if something feels off.

around.headline: BMTC issues strict guidelines to bus crews after minister's incognito ride
around.summary: Following the transport minister's surprise bus check that caught a conductor turning away a rider over exact change, BMTC has now formally instructed crews not to force passengers off buses for this reason. Use the Namma BMTC app or a UPI QR code on board to sidestep exact-change disputes.
around.source: Public TV English / The Times of India

around.headline: 1,211 motorists booked for drunk driving in Bengaluru in a single week
around.summary: Bengaluru Traffic Police booked 1,211 motorists in a week-long special drive against drunk driving. If you've had even a couple of drinks, book a cab rather than risk driving — enforcement is visibly up right now.

around.headline: Karnataka governor suspends KPSC chief over recruitment row involving his daughters
around.summary: The governor has suspended the chief of the Karnataka Public Service Commission following allegations that a recruitment process irregularly benefited his own daughters. KPSC runs many of the state government job exams residents rely on, so expect scrutiny of pending recruitment rounds.

around.headline: Supreme Court asks Centre to explain airfare rules after 'unpredictable fluctuations'
around.summary: The Supreme Court has asked the central government to place its rules on airfare pricing before it within two weeks, following concerns about unpredictable fare swings. If you're booking flights, keep comparing prices across dates rather than assuming today's fare is stable.

around.headline: Iran-US conflict escalates as Tehran retaliates against Gulf nations
around.summary: The US carried out fresh strikes on Iran, and Tehran has hit back at Gulf nations in retaliation, deepening the standoff around the Strait of Hormuz — a corridor for roughly a fifth of the world's oil shipments. A prolonged conflict there tends to push petrol/diesel prices higher, worth watching if you're budgeting for fuel.

events.name: Bengaluru Poetry Festival
events.when: Check venue listing for this edition's dates
events.note: A celebration of language, rhythm and storytelling — a good pick if you're into spoken word or literary events.

events.name: Celebrity food fest with Farah Khan, Ranveer Brar and Sunil Grover
events.note: A food festival with appearances from well-known chefs and personalities.

events.name: Awadhi Thali festival at Renaissance Bengaluru (LUSH)
events.when: Limited-time run, check venue for exact dates
events.note: A themed Awadhi cuisine spread for anyone looking to try something different for a meal out.

events.name: IIMBue 2026 — IIM Bangalore Alumni Leadership Conclave
events.note: A leadership conclave hosted by the IIM Bangalore Alumni Association — relevant if you're in the professional/alumni network.

street_smart.title: A friend confides that her husband and in-laws keep pressuring her over dowry, and she's scared to speak up. What do you do?
street_smart.based_on: Real case reported today: a Bengaluru schoolteacher died by suicide four months after her love marriage, leaving a note accusing her husband and sister-in-law of harassment, with reports pointing to dowry-related demands (India Today / The Times of India / Gulf News).
street_smart.situation: A close friend tells you, in confidence, that her in-laws keep bringing up dowry and her husband has grown cold and dismissive since the wedding. She's scared, doesn't want to 'break up the family,' and asks you not to tell anyone. What do you do?

choices.text: Respect her wish for privacy completely — just check in on her emotionally now and then, without ever mentioning any helplines or options.
choices.feedback: This feels respectful, but silence is exactly the mechanism that lets dowry harassment continue unchecked — she needs a real off-ramp, not just someone to vent to. Even the gentlest support has to include actual options, or the isolation that traps her stays intact.

choices.text: Tell her to leave the marriage immediately and cut all contact with the family, right away.
choices.feedback: Leaving is sometimes the right call, but pushing for it immediately without a safety plan, documentation, or support lined up can be dangerous and can leave her with nowhere to go. The urgency should go into building her options, not into an ultimatum.

choices.text: Gently share the Women's Helpline number (181) and the nearest police women's cell, encourage her to note down incidents with dates, and stay in touch at her pace.
choices.feedback: Correct. This gives her real institutional options and a way to build evidence, without forcing her hand or making the decision for her — and staying in touch keeps her from being isolated, which is what harassment usually depends on.

choices.text: Confront her husband or in-laws yourself to warn them off.
choices.feedback: This can escalate the danger to both her and you, and it has no legal standing — she may still have to live with them afterward, in a worse position than before. Channeling the same energy into helping her document and report is far safer.

takeaways: National Women's Helpline: 181 (24/7); Bengaluru also has Women's Helpline 1091, and every police station has a women's cell that can register a confidential complaint.

Cruelty by a husband or his relatives over dowry is a criminal offence in India (previously IPC Section 498A, now under the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita) — a documented pattern of harassment strengthens any future complaint or protection order.
Encourage keeping a dated, private record (texts, voice notes, witnessed incidents) — evidence built up over time matters more than how any one conversation is confronted.

quiz.q: Karnataka's governor suspended the chief of which state body this week, following a row over recruitment involving his own daughters?
quiz.why: KPSC conducts recruitment exams for many Karnataka state government jobs; the suspension follows allegations that the chief's own daughters benefited from irregularities in a recruitment process.

quiz.q: How many motorists did Bengaluru Traffic Police book for drunk driving in a single week-long special drive?
quiz.why: The drive is part of ongoing efforts to curb drunk-driving accidents — enforcement is visibly stepped up right now, so booking a cab after drinking is a safer bet than usual.

quiz.q: The Supreme Court this week asked the central government to do what about airfares, within two weeks?
options: Cap all domestic airfares by law
Place its rules on fare fluctuations before the court
quiz.why: The order came after concerns about 'unpredictable fluctuations' in airfares; airlines in India currently set fares dynamically without a fixed regulatory cap.

quiz.q: Which river is the primary source of piped drinking water for Bengaluru city?
quiz.why: Bengaluru draws most of its municipal water supply from the Cauvery, pumped roughly 100 km and lifted hundreds of metres in elevation — one reason water costs and shortages are a recurring civic issue in the city.

quiz.q: Under India's Right to Information (RTI) Act, within how many days must a public authority normally respond to a request?
quiz.why: The RTI Act, 2005 sets a 30-day response window (cut to 48 hours for matters concerning life or liberty), making it one of the more usable tools for ordinary citizens to hold public bodies accountable.
